description: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.

subcriteria.name: siem_platform
subcriteria.reference_text: SIEM or log aggregation platform collecting security events from infrastructure, applications, and identity providers. Splunk, Datadog, Elastic SIEM, Sumo Logic, or CloudTrail configured to ingest logs from all production systems. Dashboard showing active log sources.

subcriteria.name: alert_rules
subcriteria.reference_text: Alert rules configured for security events including failed login attempts, privilege escalation, configuration changes, and unusual access patterns. Alerts routed to PagerDuty, OpsGenie, or Slack for on-call response. Detection rules cover authentication failures, unauthorized API calls, and suspicious network activity.

subcriteria.name: log_retention
subcriteria.reference_text: Log retention policy requiring minimum 90 days online storage and 1 year archived. CloudTrail logs stored in S3 with lifecycle rules. Audit logs tamper-protected and retained per compliance requirements. Log sources include CloudTrail, VPC flow logs, and application logs.

subcriteria.name: monitoring_coverage
subcriteria.reference_text: Monitored event types include failed authentication, privilege changes, resource creation and deletion, security group modifications, and data access events. Intrusion detection providing continuous network monitoring. GuardDuty or similar threat detection active.

mismatch_signals.reason: Uptime/availability monitoring is not security event monitoring.
Application error logging without security context is not security monitoring.
